sections.title: The Budget Behind the Scrolls
sections.what: Hypatia shows Mele the Mouseion's real end: Roman fiscal strangulation, not fire. Scholars carried texts outward; exodus became the first preservation strategy.

sections.title: The Horoscope and the Translation
sections.what: Ibn al-Nadim tells Mele that al-Mamun's translation drive began with an astrological warning about a Greek rival — statecraft that outlived its own political motives.

sections.title: What the Patrons Leave Behind
sections.what: Mele connects both stops: patron ambition funds knowledge and endangers it equally — the entanglement that will shadow every library ahead.
